---
title: "CCA"
author: "Edith"
date: "2025-11-27"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
setwd("C:/Users/edith clemente arena/Desktop/EPIFITAS -GRADIENTE/treeline-epiphytes/treeline-epiphytes")
data <- read.csv("estrucPOB.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ";", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
POBSTRU <- data %>% ###TABLA DE DATOS POBLACIONALES
  select(elevation,subplot, forof,morphospe, family, dap, height, elevation, tree_str,  life_stage, l_leaf_stem, n_shoots_leaf, reprod_stage)

POBSTRU <- POBSTRU %>%
  mutate(
    tree_str = trimws(tree_str),        # elimina espacios en blanco
    tree_str = recode(tree_str,
                      "IV" = "IV",      # unificar
                      "IV " = "IV",     
                      "IV    " = "IV"),
    tree_str = factor(tree_str,
                      levels = c("I", "II", "III", "IV", "V"))
  )

table(POBSTRU$tree_str)
levels(POBSTRU$tree_str)

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(vegan)
install.packages("fastDummies")
library(fastDummies)
```


```{r}
# =====================================================
# 1. Filtrar Hymenophyllum sp1
# =====================================================
hym <- POBSTRU %>% 
  filter(morphospe == "Hymenophyllum sp1") %>%
  drop_na(l_leaf_stem, n_shoots_leaf, life_stage, reprod_stage,
          elevation, dap, height, tree_str)

# =====================================================
# 2. Crear dummies con tus niveles reales
# =====================================================

hym2 <- hym %>%
  mutate(
    life_stage = factor(life_stage, levels = c("juvenil", "adulto")),
    reprod_stage = factor(reprod_stage, levels = c("esteril", "reproductivo")),
    tree_str = factor(tree_str, levels = c("I","II","III","IV","V"))
  ) %>%
  dummy_cols(
    select_columns = c("life_stage", "reprod_stage", "tree_str"),
    remove_selected_columns = TRUE
  )

# =====================================================
# 3. Matriz Y (respuesta poblacional)
# =====================================================

vars_resp <- c(
  "l_leaf_stem",
  "n_shoots_leaf",
  "life_stage_adulto",
  "reprod_stage_reproductivo"
)

Y <- hym2[, vars_resp]

# =====================================================
# 4. Matriz X (ambiental)
# =====================================================

vars_env <- c(
  "elevation",
  "dap",
  "height",
  grep("tree_str_", names(hym2), value = TRUE)
)

X <- hym2[, vars_env]

# Escalar variables continuas
X[, c("elevation", "dap", "height")] <- scale(X[, c("elevation", "dap", "height")])

# =====================================================
# 5. RDA final
# =====================================================

mod_rda <- rda(Y ~ ., data = X)

# =====================================================
# 6. Significancia
# =====================================================

anova(mod_rda)               # Modelo completo
anova(mod_rda, by = "axis")  # Ejes
anova(mod_rda, by = "term")  # Variables

# =====================================================
# 7. Gráfico simple (versión limpia)
# =====================================================

sites <- scores(mod_rda, display = "sites", scaling = 2)
env <- scores(mod_rda, display = "bp", scaling = 2)

plot(mod_rda, display = "sites", type = "n", scaling = 2)
points(sites, pch = 21, bg = "lightblue", col = "black", cex = 0.8)

arrows(0, 0, env[,1], env[,2], col = "red", lwd = 1.5, length = 0.1)
text(env[,1], env[,2], labels = rownames(env),
     col = "red", pos = 3, cex = 1)

title("RDA – Hymenophyllum sp.1 (estructura poblacional)")


```

```{r}
##############################################################
###   RDA – Hymenophyllum sp1 (estructura poblacional)     ###
###        SIN variable tree_str (optimizado)              ###
##############################################################

library(dplyr)
library(vegan)
library(fastDummies)
library(ggplot2)

##############################################################
# 1. Filtrar solo la especie y eliminar NA
##############################################################

hym <- POBSTRU %>% 
  filter(morphospe == "Hymenophyllum sp1") %>%
  drop_na(l_leaf_stem, 
          n_shoots_leaf, 
          life_stage, 
          reprod_stage,
          elevation, 
          dap, 
          height)

##############################################################
# 2. Convertir variables categóricas a dummies
##############################################################

hym2 <- hym %>%
  mutate(
    life_stage = factor(life_stage, levels = c("juvenil", "adulto")),
    reprod_stage = factor(reprod_stage, levels = c("esteril", "reproductivo"))
  ) %>%
  dummy_cols(
    select_columns = c("life_stage", "reprod_stage"),
    remove_selected_columns = TRUE
  )

##############################################################
# 3. Matriz de respuesta Y (variables poblacionales)
##############################################################

vars_resp <- c(
  "l_leaf_stem",
  "n_shoots_leaf",
  "life_stage_adulto",
  "reprod_stage_reproductivo"
)

Y <- hym2[, vars_resp]

##############################################################
# 4. Matriz ambiental X (SIN tree_str)
##############################################################

vars_env <- c("elevation", "dap", "height")

X <- hym2[, vars_env]

# Escalar variables ambientales
X <- as.data.frame(scale(X))

##############################################################
# 5. Ejecutar el RDA
##############################################################

mod_rda <- rda(Y ~ ., data = X)

##############################################################
# 6. Pruebas de significancia
##############################################################

cat("\n### ANOVA MODELO COMPLETO ###\n")
print(anova(mod_rda))

cat("\n### ANOVA POR EJE ###\n")
print(anova(mod_rda, by = "axis"))

cat("\n### ANOVA POR VARIABLE ###\n")
print(anova(mod_rda, by = "term"))

##############################################################
# 7. Gráfico base R (simple)
##############################################################

sites <- scores(mod_rda, display = "sites", scaling = 2)
env   <- scores(mod_rda, display = "bp",    scaling = 2)

plot(mod_rda, display = "sites", type = "n", scaling = 2)
points(sites, pch = 21, bg = "lightblue", col = "black", cex = 0.7)

arrows(0, 0, env[,1], env[,2], col = "red", lwd = 1.5, length = 0.12)
text(env[,1], env[,2], labels = rownames(env), 
     col = "red", pos = 3, cex = 1)

title("RDA – Hymenophyllum sp.1 (estructura poblacional)")

##############################################################
# 8. Gráfico bonito con ggplot2
##############################################################

scr_sites <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda, display = "sites", scaling = 2))
scr_env   <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda, display = "bp", scaling = 2))

scr_env$label <- rownames(scr_env)

ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = scr_sites,
             aes(x = RDA1, y = RDA2),
             alpha = 0.3, size = 1.2, color = "darkblue") +
  geom_segment(data = scr_env,
               aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = RDA1, yend = RDA2),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
               color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(data = scr_env,
            aes(x = RDA1, y = RDA2, label = label),
            color = "red", fontface = "bold", vjust = -0.6) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "RDA – Hymenophyllum sp.1 (estructura poblacional)",
    x = "RDA1",
    y = "RDA2"
  )

```
eSCALAR AMBAS MATRICES porque hay muchos outliers 
```{r}
# Escalar la matriz Y (respuesta)
Y_scaled <- as.data.frame(scale(Y))

# RDA con Y escalado
mod_rda <- rda(Y_scaled ~ ., data = X)

# Nuevos scores
scr_sites <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda, display="sites", scaling=2))
scr_env   <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda, display="bp", scaling=2))
scr_env$label <- rownames(scr_env)

# Nuevo gráfico
ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = scr_sites,
             aes(x = RDA1, y = RDA2),
             alpha = 0.4, size = 1, color = "darkblue") +
  geom_segment(data = scr_env,
               aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = RDA1, yend = RDA2),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
               color = "red", linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_text(data = scr_env,
            aes(x = RDA1, y = RDA2, label = label),
            color = "red", fontface = "bold", vjust = -0.6) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "RDA – Hymenophyllum sp.1 (estructura poblacional)",
    x = "RDA1",
    y = "RDA2"
  )

```
quitando outliers
```{r}
summary(Y_scaled)
which(Y_scaled$n_shoots_leaf > 6)
hym2[which(Y_scaled$n_shoots_leaf > 6), ]

# Crear una copia limpia para el análisis
hym_rda <- hym2[-c(385, 410, 427), ]
##RECONSTRUIR MATRICES Y Y X
Y <- hym_rda[, vars_resp]      # variables poblacionales
X <- hym_rda[, vars_env]       # variables ambientales

# Escalar ambas matrices
Y_scaled <- as.data.frame(scale(Y))
X_scaled <- as.data.frame(scale(X))
#RDA
mod_rda <- rda(Y_scaled ~ ., data = X_scaled)
#Grafico 
scr_sites <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda, display="sites", scaling=2))
scr_env   <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda, display="bp", scaling=2))

scr_env$label <- rownames(scr_env)

hy_rda <-ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = scr_sites,
             aes(x = RDA1, y = RDA2),
             alpha = 0.4, size = 1.2, color = "darkblue") +
  geom_segment(data = scr_env,
               aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = RDA1, yend = RDA2),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
               color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(data = scr_env,
            aes(x = RDA1, y = RDA2, label = label),
            color = "red", fontface = "bold", vjust = -0.6) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "RDA – Hymenophyllum sp.1 ",
    x = "RDA1",
    y = "RDA2"
  )
ggsave("RDA_hysp1.png",
       plot = hy_rda,
       width = 18,      # ancho en cm
       height = 14,     # alto en cm
       dpi = 600,       # resolución alta (revista)
       units = "cm")
##quitar unos outliers mas
#OPCIÓN A: Eliminar outliers solo para la figura
outliers_idx <- which(abs(scr_sites$RDA1) > 4 | abs(scr_sites$RDA2) > 4)
outliers_idx
scr_sites_clean <- scr_sites %>%
  filter(abs(RDA1) <= 4, abs(RDA2) <= 4)
p_rda <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = scr_sites_clean,
             aes(x = RDA1, y = RDA2),
             alpha = 0.5, size = 1.3, color = "darkblue") +
  geom_segment(data = scr_env,
               aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = RDA1, yend = RDA2),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
               color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(data = scr_env,
            aes(x = RDA1, y = RDA2, label = label),
            color = "red", fontface = "bold", vjust = -0.6) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "RDA – Hymenophyllum sp.1",
    x = "RDA1",
    y = "RDA2"
  )
ggsave("RDA_Hymenophyllum_clean.png",
       plot = p_rda,
       width = 18, height = 14, dpi = 600, units = "cm")






# Pruebas de significancia
cat("\n### ANOVA MODELO COMPLETO ###\n")
print(anova(mod_rda))

cat("\n### ANOVA POR EJE ###\n")
print(anova(mod_rda, by = "axis"))

cat("\n### ANOVA POR VARIABLE ###\n")
print(anova(mod_rda, by = "term"))
```
RDA TODAS LAS ESPECIES JUNTAS PERO CON VARIABLES CATEGORICAS
```{r}
##1️⃣ Crear la matriz de respuesta Y para todas las especies
library(dplyr)
library(fastDummies)

# 1. Usar TODOS los individuos del POBSTRU
datos <- POBSTRU %>%
  drop_na(l_leaf_stem, n_shoots_leaf, life_stage, reprod_stage,
          elevation, dap, height)

# 2. Convertir estados a variables dummy
datos2 <- datos %>%
  mutate(
    life_stage = factor(life_stage, levels = c("juvenil", "adulto")),
    reprod_stage = factor(reprod_stage, levels = c("esteril", "reproductivo"))
  ) %>%
  fastDummies::dummy_cols(
    select_columns = c("life_stage", "reprod_stage"),
    remove_selected_columns = TRUE
  )

# 3. Matriz de respuesta Y (TODOS LOS INDIVIDUOS)
Y <- datos2 %>% select(
  l_leaf_stem,
  n_shoots_leaf,
  life_stage_adulto,
  reprod_stage_reproductivo
)
#2️⃣ Crear la matriz ambiental X
X <- datos2 %>% select(
  elevation,
  dap,
  height
)
#3️⃣ Escalar ambas matrices
Y_scaled <- scale(Y)
X_scaled <- scale(X)
##4️⃣ Ajustar un único RDA para TODAS las epífitas
library(vegan)
mod_rda_all <- rda(Y_scaled ~ ., data = as.data.frame(X_scaled))
#5️⃣ Resumen del modelo
summary(mod_rda_all)
anova(mod_rda_all)
anova(mod_rda_all, by = "axis")
anova(mod_rda_all, by = "term")
# ===============================
# 6️⃣ SCORES PARA GRAFICAR (UNA SOLA VEZ)
# ===============================

scr_sites <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda_all, display = "sites"))
scr_env   <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda_all, display = "bp"))

# añadir elevation (MISMO ORDEN que datos2 / Y)
scr_sites$elevation <- datos2$elevation

# ===============================
# 7️⃣ FILTRAR OUTLIERS
# ===============================

scr_sites_clean <- scr_sites %>%
  filter(abs(RDA1) < 4, abs(RDA2) < 4)

# ===============================
# 8️⃣ BIPLOT FINAL
# ===============================

library(ggplot2)

p_rda_all <- ggplot() +
  
  geom_point(
    data = scr_sites_clean,
    aes(x = RDA1, y = RDA2, color = elevation),
    alpha = 0.6, size = 2
  ) +
  
  geom_segment(
    data = scr_env,
    aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = RDA1, yend = RDA2),
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
    color = "red", linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  geom_text(
    data = scr_env,
    aes(x = RDA1, y = RDA2, label = rownames(scr_env)),
    color = "red", vjust = -0.6, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  scale_color_viridis_c(name = "Elevation (m)") +
  
  labs(
    x = "RDA1",
    y = "RDA2",
    title = "RDA biplot of population traits across all epiphytic species"
  ) +
  
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

p_rda_all
names(scr_sites_clean)

ggsave("RDA_all_epiphytes.png",
       plot = p_rda_all,
       width = 20, height = 16, dpi = 600, units = "cm")

```
SIN CATEGORICAS
```{r}
##1️⃣ Preparar datos (solo variables necesarias)
library(dplyr)
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)

# Usar SOLO individuos con datos completos
datos <- POBSTRU %>%
  drop_na(
    l_leaf_stem,
    n_shoots_leaf,
    elevation,
    dap,
    height
  )
##2️⃣ Matriz de respuesta Y (SOLO rasgos continuos)
Y <- datos %>%
  select(
    l_leaf_stem,
    n_shoots_leaf
  )
##3️⃣ Matriz ambiental X
X <- datos %>% select(
  elevation,
  dap,
  height
)
### 4️⃣ Escalar matrices
Y_scaled <- scale(Y)
X_scaled <- scale(X)
##5️⃣ Ajustar el modelo RDA
mod_rda_cont <- rda(Y_scaled ~ elevation + dap + height,
                    data = as.data.frame(X_scaled))
##6️⃣ Evaluación estadística
# Modelo global
anova(mod_rda_cont, permutations = 999)

# Por ejes
anova(mod_rda_cont, by = "axis", permutations = 999)

# Por variable
anova(mod_rda_cont, by = "term", permutations = 999)

##7️⃣ Extraer scores para el biplot
# Sitios (individuos)
scr_sites <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda_cont, display = "sites"))

# Variables ambientales (flechas)
scr_env <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda_cont, display = "bp"))

# Añadir elevación para colorear
scr_sites$elevation <- datos$elevation

##8️⃣ Biplot limpio y publicable (ggplot)
p_rda_cont <- ggplot() +
  
  # Individuos
  geom_point(
    data = scr_sites,
    aes(x = RDA1, y = RDA2, color = elevation),
    alpha = 0.6,
    size = 2
  ) +
  
  # Flechas ambientales
  geom_segment(
    data = scr_env,
    aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = RDA1, yend = RDA2),
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
    color = "red",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  # Etiquetas de flechas
  geom_text(
    data = scr_env,
    aes(x = RDA1, y = RDA2, label = rownames(scr_env)),
    color = "red",
    fontface = "bold",
    vjust = -0.6
  ) +
  
  scale_color_viridis_c(name = "Elevation (m)") +
  
  labs(
    x = "RDA1",
    y = "RDA2",
    title = "RDA of continuous population traits across epiphytic species"
  ) +
  
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

p_rda_cont

ggsave("RDA_all_epiphytes.png",
       plot = p_rda_cont,
       width = 20, height = 16, dpi = 600, units = "cm")

```
ESCALAR X Y LOGARITMIZAR Y FACTOR elev
```{r}
datos$elev_factor <- factor(datos$elevation,
                            levels = sort(unique(datos$elevation)))

mod_rda_cont_f <- rda(
  Y_scaled ~ elev_factor + dap + height,
  data = datos
)
####escalar x por variable
datos$dap_z    <- scale(datos$dap)
datos$height_z <- scale(datos$height)

mod_rda_cont_f <- rda(
  Y_scaled ~ elev_factor + dap_z + height_z,
  data = datos
)
#######logaritmizar
Y_log <- datos %>%
  transmute(
    l_leaf_stem_log = log(l_leaf_stem),
    n_shoots_leaf_log = log1p(n_shoots_leaf)  # log(1 + x)
  )
Y_log_scaled <- scale(Y_log)

mod_rda_cont_f <- rda(
  Y_log_scaled ~ elev_factor + dap_z + height_z,
  data = datos
)

##########################
anova(mod_rda_cont_f, permutations = 999)
anova(mod_rda_cont_f, by = "term", permutations = 999)
anova(mod_rda_cont_f, by = "axis", permutations = 999)
# Scores de los individuos
scr_sites <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda_cont_f, display = "sites"))

# Vectores ambientales (dap, height, elev_factor)
scr_env <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda_cont_f, display = "bp"))

# Centroides de variables categóricas (elev_factor)
scr_cent <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda_cont_f, display = "cn"))
############## Flechas SOLO continuas
scr_env <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda_cont_f, display = "bp"))
scr_env <- scr_env[rownames(scr_env) %in% c("dap", "height"), ]

# Centroides del factor elevación
scr_cent <- as.data.frame(scores(mod_rda_cont_f, display = "cn"))


# Añadir elevación como factor
scr_sites$elev_factor <- datos$elev_factor
lims <- scr_sites %>%
  summarise(
    x_low = quantile(RDA1, 0.01),
    x_high = quantile(RDA1, 0.99),
    y_low = quantile(RDA2, 0.01),
    y_high = quantile(RDA2, 0.99)
  )

scr_sites_clean <- scr_sites %>%
  filter(
    RDA1 > lims$x_low, RDA1 < lims$x_high,
    RDA2 > lims$y_low, RDA2 < lims$y_high
  )

p_rda_final <- ggplot() +

  # Individuos
  geom_point(
    data = scr_sites_clean,
    aes(RDA1, RDA2, color = elev_factor),
    alpha = 0.7,
    size = 2
  ) +

  # Centroides de elevación
  geom_point(
    data = scr_cent,
    aes(RDA1, RDA2),
    shape = 4,
    size = 5,
    stroke = 2
  ) +

  geom_text(
    data = scr_cent,
    aes(RDA1, RDA2, label = rownames(scr_cent)),
    fontface = "bold",
    vjust = -0.8
  ) +

  # Flechas ambientales
  geom_segment(
    data = scr_env,
    aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = RDA1, yend = RDA2),
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
    color = "red",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +

  geom_text(
    data = scr_env,
    aes(RDA1, RDA2, label = rownames(scr_env)),
    color = "red",
    fontface = "bold",
    vjust = -0.6
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "RDA of continuous epiphyte population traits",
    x = "RDA1",
    y = "RDA2",
    color = "Elevation"
  ) +

  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

p_rda_final

```
aplicar jitter grafico

```{r}
# Copia de los scores (NO afecta el análisis)
scr_sites_plot <- scr_sites_clean

# Aplicar jitter suave
set.seed(123)  # reproducible
scr_sites_plot$RDA1 <- jitter(scr_sites_plot$RDA1, amount = 0.03)
scr_sites_plot$RDA2 <- jitter(scr_sites_plot$RDA2, amount = 0.03)
library(ggplot2)

ggplot() +

  # Individuos con jitter
  geom_point(
    data = scr_sites_plot,
    aes(RDA1, RDA2, color = elev_factor),
    alpha = 0.6,
    size = 2
  ) +

  # Centroides de elevación
  geom_point(
    data = scr_cent,
    aes(RDA1, RDA2),
    shape = 4,
    size = 5,
    stroke = 2
  ) +

  geom_text(
    data = scr_cent,
    aes(RDA1, RDA2, label = rownames(scr_cent)),
    fontface = "bold",
    vjust = -0.7
  ) +

  # Flechas SOLO continuas
  geom_segment(
    data = scr_env,
    aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = RDA1, yend = RDA2),
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
    color = "red",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +

  geom_text(
    data = scr_env,
    aes(RDA1, RDA2, label = rownames(scr_env)),
    color = "red",
    fontface = "bold",
    vjust = -0.6
  ) +

  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "RDA of continuous epiphyte population traits",
    x = "RDA1",
    y = "RDA2",
    color = "Elevation (m)"
  )

```
